why_not_publishable_yet: The local evidence is trust-surface rich but event-poor: no public vulnerability, bounded incident writeup, or official remediation notice ties the cloud-agent execution/dependency/plugin boundary to a concrete failure event.
minimum_threshold_changing_source: One public vulnerability/advisory, incident writeup, or official remediation notice tied to Copilot cloud-agent MCP, allowlist, package-registry, or browser-download trust boundaries.
expected_paper_readiness_delta: Would remove the most reviewer-visible mechanism-coverage zero state and let Table 6 show at least one bounded trust-boundary row beyond execution, exfiltration, memory/state, and identity abuse.
negative_result_to_preserve: Do not promote provider docs, workflow blogs, or provenance-adjacent traces into seeded security evidence without incident-grade public support.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Gap-to-first-row funnel; mechanism-diversity heatmap; Route-B blocker panel.
claim_support_or_thesis_use: Methods/limitations note explaining why high-stakes agent supply-chain surfaces are visible in local corpora before incident-grade evidence exists.

why_not_publishable_yet: The current package has the strongest non-vendor independence here, but no incident-specific peer-reviewed or conference-grade case analysis maps cleanly to the live CVEs; local scholarly hits are framework-overlap near misses only.
minimum_threshold_changing_source: One peer-reviewed paper, workshop paper, or conference-grade technical case writeup mapping directly to CVE-2023-29374 or CVE-2024-3271.
expected_paper_readiness_delta: Would remove the package's authority-balance zero state without adding a new weak row, giving the article and thesis one reviewer-safe scholarly support layer inside the seeded security family.
negative_result_to_preserve: Do not count framework-overlap papers with different CVEs or different vulnerable APIs as row-specific peer-reviewed support.
dashboard_or_figure_hook: Authority-balance panel; evidence-layer Sankey; limitations-to-upgrade ladder.
claim_support_or_thesis_use: Methods/results caution that scholarly overlap and incident-specific support are separate evidence layers.

why_not_publishable_yet: This row would broaden mechanism diversity and authority balance at once, but no incident-specific public scholarly/security-paper treatment of CVE-2026-44843 or the same vulnerable path is attached locally.
minimum_threshold_changing_source: One peer-reviewed or conference-grade technical treatment of unsafe deserialization, runtime memory contamination, or attacker-controlled state revival tied directly to CVE-2026-44843 or the same vulnerable LangChain runtime path.
expected_paper_readiness_delta: Would strengthen a reviewer-safe argument that LIMEN's seeded security package includes more than execution or exfiltration even at the scholarly-support layer.

dashboard_or_figure_hook: Mechanism-diversity heatmap; authority-balance panel; limitations-to-upgrade ladder.
